title: Hemionitis glabella
intro: Hemionitis glabella, also known as the Glabella fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of wounds, infections, and diseases such as cancer. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis glabella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hemionitis glabella is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used in the production of extracts and supplements.
